log_entries.thought_preview: The sample overview confirms this is a PE32 executable tagged as a 'spreader' with runtime modules and an overlay. The size is 159KB. I have 3 sandboxes available (CAPA, Cuckoofork, Jujubox). I should

The IOC summary shows no network activity, no registry persistence keys, but 2 dropped files and 0 processes created (likely because the main process wasn't spawned in the sandbox, only the dropped fi
The dropped files show two executables: one in a fake Mozilla directory (C:\PROGRA~3\Mozilla\zhsrrgd.exe) and one in the current directory (ctvqzym.exe). The first one is likely a dropper or payload,

The registry changes show no persistence keys, which is interesting given the scheduled task 'hiqvsnd.job' was written. Scheduled tasks are a form of persistence but may not be captured as registry ke

The process activity shows no processes created (likely because the main process didn't spawn in the sandbox) but lists notable modules including shell32.dll, ole32.dll, and rpcrt4.dll, which are comm

The Cuckoofork sandbox executed the sample, dropped 1 file, wrote 2 files, loaded 11 modules, and has a PCAP. This confirms the sample is dynamic and behaves differently than the static analysis sugge

The Jujubox sandbox shows different behavior: 11 registry keys opened, 1 file dropped, 1 file written, and only 1 module loaded. This suggests the sample might have different behaviors in different sa

The network activity tool shows no IPs, domains, or HTTP conversations, which contradicts the Cuckoofork sandbox summary indicating it has a PCAP. This might mean the network activity was internal, en

I have gathered all available data. The sample is a PE32 executable (159KB) compiled with LCC-Win32, tagged as a 'spreader' with runtime modules and an overlay. Key findings:
